programme.title: An Optimist's Guide to the Planet
programme.desc: Nikolaj begins his global journey to find out how to tell dusk from dawn in the age of climate change; from the scientists behind carbon neutral jet fuel to the oceanographer creating cloud cover for the Great Barrier Reef, the answer becomes clear.

programme.title: An Optimist's Guide to the Planet
programme.desc: Shelter is one of humanity's most basic needs; but as the global population grows, so does the construction industry's environmental footprint.

programme.title: An Optimist's Guide to the Planet
programme.desc: Nikolaj Coster-Waldau begins season two and we're reminded of the delicate balance between human progress and protecting our planet.

programme.title: An Optimist's Guide to the Planet
programme.desc: Nikolaj travels the world to meet people transforming waste into resources; showing that waste isn't something to hide -- it's something we can rethink as valuable raw material.

programme.title: An Optimist's Guide to the Planet
programme.desc: From transforming concrete into a carbon sink to mining nickel from genetically enhanced plants, we meet the bold inventors preserving and adapting to nature's offerings to help humanity thrive.

programme.title: An Optimist's Guide to the Planet
programme.desc: Meeting humanity's insatiable demand for energy while still curbing greenhouse emissions will require creative solutions both big and small.

programme.title: Leaders with Francine Lacqua: Alexander Stubb, President of Finland
programme.desc: Francine Lacqua visits Finland, where the threat from neighboring Russia looms large; she speaks to President Alexander Stubb about Finnish resilience and how his country is preparing for an uncertain future.

programme.title: LEADERS With Francine Lacqua
programme.sub-title: Ray Dalio, Founder of Bridgewater Associates
programme.desc: Francine Lacqua meets Ray Dalio on OceanX in the port of Nice for the UN Ocean Conference. They speak about radical transparency, building and leaving one of the largest hedge funds and what's next.

programme.title: LEADERS With Francine Lacqua
programme.sub-title: How to Run Your Family's $1.6 Trillion Empire
programme.desc: Francine Lacqua talks with Franklin Templeton CEO Jenny Johnson about how to steer a $1.6 trillion business amid the rise of AI, shifting politics and booming private markets.

programme.title: Power Players
programme.sub-title: Week in the Life of an F1 Driver
programme.desc: Jason Kelly follows Pierre Gasly from Alpine Headquarters to a Paris photo shoot to get a firsthand look at how the business of being an F1 driver goes far beyond the track.

programme.title: Power Players
programme.desc: Jason Kelly heads to Maranello, Italy, to speak with Ferrari's leadership about the coming regulatory changes for Formula 1 and how Ferrari's racing technology informs the road cars of today.

programme.title: Power Players
programme.desc: Jason Kelly visits the UK's Motorsport Valley, where Williams Team Principal James Volwes and F1 Academy drivers' champion Abbi Pulling show how advanced simulation is shaping the future of Formula 1.

programme.title: The Infinite Explorer With Hannah Fry
programme.desc: Hannah Fry visits South Korea to explore how it became a cultural and tech superpower; she trains with a Taekwondo black belt, learns kimchi secrets from a grandmaster and dives into pro gaming.

programme.title: The Infinite Explorer With Hannah Fry
programme.desc: Hannah Fry reveals why ancient Greece was the birthplace of so many big ideas; she decodes the Parthenon's geometry, sails the Aegean with ancient tools and is swept up in a riotous festival.

programme.title: The Future with Hannah Fry
programme.desc: Human survival depends on water, yet global reserves are rapidly shrinking; it's now replaced oil as the most likely cause of global conflict; discussing whether technology can help secure humanity's future water needs.

programme.title: The Future with Hannah Fry
programme.desc: Pain is a fact of life; for some, it is excruciating and inescapable; but amid the continuing fallout of the opioid crisis, the development of new technologies aims to limit and even eliminate pain.

programme.title: The Future with Hannah Fry
programme.desc: Soon, another billion people will be online, mostly from developing nations; ways new users will embrace this technology to transform their lives; its meaning for the rest of the world.

programme.title: The Circuit With Emily Chang

programme.title: Sex-for-Work Claims in West Africa's Rubber Industry
programme.desc: A company majority-owned by billionaire Vincent Bolloré's Bolloré Group and Hubert Fabri is the subject of allegations of labor abuses at some of its African rubber plantations, including by female workers who say they were forced to engage in sex.

programme.title: Bloomberg Investigates: How a Ugandan Clinic Exposed a Private Healthcare Tragedy
programme.desc: In a Kampala slum, a group of midwives help vulnerable mothers give birth for free. Under a mile away, a private hospital is out of reach for most of the community, with devastating consequences.

programme.title: Bloomberg Investigates
programme.desc: The demand for human eggs is booming, giving rise to a largely unregulated and dangerous marketplace; in India, meeting a teenage girl caught up in this fertility underworld.

programme.title: Bloomberg Investigates
programme.desc: Fake permits, undercover informants and millions of dollars are involved; a US government agency works to prove suppliers to research labs import wild monkeys from Cambodia with false paperwork.

programme.title: Bloomberg Investigates
programme.desc: An estimated $1 trillion lost globally to fraud; with law enforcement struggling to cope, scambaiter vigilantes are filling the gaps.

programme.title: Bloomberg Investigates

programme.desc: Despite Anubis' previous failure to follow Talpa's orders, Talpa dispatches him to an amusement park where the heroes have gathered; Kento tries to fight without his armor, fearing that using it will turn him against his friends.

programme.title: Ronin Warriors
programme.desc: The Ancient One reveals the nature of the mystical armors and their relationship to good and evil; as Anubis rethinks his loyalty to Talpa, Talpa's other warlords launch an attack.

programme.title: Lupin the Third Part 2
programme.desc: After capturing the world's largest emerald, Lupin hides it in a museum to retrieve at a later time.

programme.title: Lupin the Third Part 2
programme.desc: The Marukin Safe Company is forced into bankruptcy after Lupin cracks open their impenetrable safe; to test his own skills, Marukin's son challenges Lupin to open a safe that he has designed.

programme.title: Lupin the Third Part 2
programme.desc: While trying to steal General Hatler's prize medallion, Lupin inadvertently reels in Hatler's biggest secret, his toupee; an enraged General Hatler orders Lupin and his gang to be killed.

programme.title: Lupin the Third Part 2
programme.desc: Upon returning to Japan, Goemon finds his master on the brink of death; Jinkuro, a fellow disciple, steals half of the scroll containing the secret arts of the Iga Ninja.

programme.title: Great Teacher Onizuka
programme.desc: A student challenges Onizuka to a fight after learning his mother is dating the teacher.

programme.title: Great Teacher Onizuka
programme.desc: Onizuka gets his hand stuck in a bowling ball, leading to unsupervised students going bungee jumping. Animated.

programme.title: Great Teacher Onizuka
programme.sub-title: Onizuka and the Art of War
programme.desc: Onizuka sparks a long-running feud between colleagues.

programme.title: Great Teacher Onizuka
programme.desc: Onizuka tries to help an outcast female student to build more self-esteem.

programme.title: Great Teacher Onizuka
programme.sub-title: To Be Idolized by a Nation
programme.desc: Onizuka encourages a student to audition for a hit television series.

programme.title: Space Adventure COBRA
programme.desc: Cobra and Dominique escape Planet Lelouge and the Snow Gorillas. They learn the meaning behind the Nelson sisters' tattoos and attempt to uncover the location of Captain Nelson's treasure.

programme.title: Space Adventure COBRA
programme.desc: On Zados, a harsh, desert planet, Captain Nelson's lost treasure lies hidden. Rumor has it that the treasure is actually the Ultimate Weapon of the universe. Cobra desperately tries to make it to the weapon before the Snow Gorilla leader Sandra does.

programme.title: Space Adventure COBRA
programme.desc: Sandra has the Ultimate Weapon and doesn't hesitate to unleash its full power on Cobra. He'll need to use his wits if he's to have any hope of defeating her.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Chef Andrew Zimmern prepares a traditional Northern Italian-style wild boar ragu. Paired with a hearty bowl of pasta. Next, chef Zimmern blackens beautiful crimson filets of steelhead in a screaming hot cast iron pan.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Chef Zimmern cooks his version of Peking-style goose. Next, Chef Zimmern cooks a meal of striped bass.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Braised Pigeon with Homemade Herb Gnocchi

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: For pig roast perfection, Chef Andrew Zimmern marinates a whole hog.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Chef Andrew Zimmern starts simmering a classic Italian Bolognese sauce, with ground bison, lots of red wine and tomatoes. Then, he adds marinated bison skirt steak tacos with borracho beans to the tailgate menu.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Chef Andrew prepares a crave-able appetizer of bison tenderloin bites served with tamarind sauce and a classic Hunan peanut sauce. Then chef grills prosciutto-wrapped whole trout stuffed with lemon and herbs served with rice pilaf.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Grilled Clams Casino and Garlicky Clam Pizza
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern celebrates cherrystone clams with two show-stopping recipes. He prepares classic grilled clams casino with pancetta, then pushes boundaries with an innovative grilled pizza topped with clams, garlic, chiles, and cheese.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: Winter citrus is here to brighten up your day. Frankie is fixing up some light, zesty, and affordable recipes to add a little seasonal sunshine to your plate.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: From casual weeknights to special occasions, be the star of the dinnertime with these crowd-pleasing meals.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: Put leftovers to good use with tasty, waste-reducing recipes that turn excess food into exciting new dishes.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: Treat yourself to a five star pasta dinner, as Frankie cooks his signature dishes with showstopping sauces.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.sub-title: I Need To Eat Right Now
programme.desc: At the end of a long, hectic day, when you need dinner in a pinch, Frankie unlocks his not-so-secret formula for whipping up quick and easy meals, using minimal ingredients that will satisfy your cravings and feed the family in a flash.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: There may be infinite ways to define pie, but the only math that matters is what makes these sweet and savory dishes fit the bill. In true Struggle Style, Frankie rolls out his best tips and tricks for enjoying hassle free, cost friendly pies.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: Brackets are complete and all that's missing is food to fuel your team's run. Huddle around the kitchen as Frankie preps the ultimate watch party spread. These savory, spicy and sweet dishes are championship-worthy even if your bracket goes bust.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: As one of the cheapest members of the produce aisle, bananas will save you bunches of money when you whip up these delightfully appealing recipes that go above and beyond a basic bread.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: Frankie takes on his longtime frenemy, the onion, and all its powerful relatives. There might be a few tears shed along the way, but they are well worth the delicious and flavorful results, from sweet buttery leeks to the ultimate onion masterpiece.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: Frankie is all about BYOL - bring your own lunch. It's not only good for your wallet, it's good for your health. Skip the vending machine and the cafeteria in favor of creative, cost effective and easy-to-transport midday meals.

programme.title: Let Frankie Cook
programme.sub-title: Pancake Stacks and Snacks for Two
programme.desc: Frankie flips the script for his wife Heather making breakfast for dinner featuring Banana Maple Pancakes, Eggs Benedict with prosciutto cotto, Home Fries with Bloody Mary Ketchup, Sausage Scrapple, and a boozy Espresso Milkshake.

programme.title: Let Frankie Cook
programme.desc: Frankie reunites his old band for a jam session and a spin on a college-era classic - featuring Shoyu Ramen with Tare Eggs, sweet and savory Chicken Meatballs, Seaweed Cucumber Salad, and a crispy Okonomiyaki pancake.

programme.title: Let Frankie Cook
programme.desc: Frankie skips the stress and roasts a juicy spatchcocked turkey, then goes big on sides-cardamom rolls, Sweet & Savory Dauphinoise Potatoes, Brussels Sprouts with Bacon Jam, and a bright Citrus Salad with smoked salt.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: Seasons
programme.desc: Jamie kicks off autumn with Italian-inspired cheesy cabbage pasta and smoky sweetcorn burgers with feta. He shares tips for storing onions and saving tomato seeds, finishing with a beautiful tomato galette for a perfect fall meal.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: Seasons
programme.sub-title: Autumn's Harvest: Roast Chicken and Tarte Tatin
programme.desc: Jamie cooks a roast chicken with eggplant, bakes feta with figs & prosciutto, and creates a runner bean stew with fish. He shares tips for storing berries and chiles, finishing with a stunning pear tarte tatin.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: Seasons
programme.sub-title: Autumn's Last Bites: Squash Tagliatelle and Blackberry Cobbler
programme.desc: Jamie wraps up autumn with cheesy squash tagliatelle, a mushroom curry with crispy ginger rice, and celeriac remoulade. He forages for blackberries to make a cobbler, the perfect end to a crisp autumn day.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver's Christmas Shortcuts

programme.title: Jamie Oliver's Christmas Shortcuts

programme.sub-title: How Do You Like Them Apples

programme.title: Struggle Meals

programme.title: Struggle Meals

programme.title: Struggle Meals

programme.title: Struggle Meals

programme.title: Struggle Meals

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: Fast & Simple
programme.sub-title: Jarred Pepper Pasta and Sheet Pan Brisket
programme.desc: Jamie cooks a speedy jarred-pepper pasta, crispy chicken in baked-bread sauce, and a beef brisket traybake - all prepped in minutes. He makes shelf-raid spiced haricot beans, and rounds it all off with a fast and simple strawberry & balsamic tart.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: Fast & Simple
programme.sub-title: Creamy Chicken Pot Pie and Chocolate Tiramisu
programme.desc: Jamie cooks a midweek traybake of roast veg and Camembert. He turns poached chicken into a creamy chicken & chive pie, serves up his easy green chilli salsa under sizzling butterflied prawns, and finishes it all off with decadent chocolate tiramisù.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: Fast & Simple
programme.desc: Jamie shares a simple salmon dish perfect for a midweek meal, and some no-fuss fish cakes made from tins. He cooks a lamb Madras traybake, as well as a no-cook pasta sauce, and crowns it all off with a hot and crispy ice cream parcel!

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: Fast & Simple
programme.desc: Jamie cooks a speedy midweek chicken traybake. He shows us cool ways to upgrade salmon, knocks up a shelf-raid quesadilla toastie, and transforms a batch-cook Bolognese into cheesy bread bowls. All finished off with a super simple banoffee tart.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: Fast & Simple
programme.sub-title: Smash Burger and Creamy Chicken with Mushrooms
programme.desc: Jamie cooks a midweek meal of speedy sweet pea chicken, a juicy Smash burger and a chicken and mushroom rice traybake. He batch-cooks his seven-veg sauce, followed by a spicy fish soup. And finishes off with a perfect pudding of tinned fruit granita.

programme.title: Impossible Builds
programme.desc: While on holiday in the Outer Hebrides, Ruth Hamilton and John Henry fell in love with the isolated wilderness of the Isle of Harris. To make their new home possible, the couple found an innovative construction company, who specialize in kit houses.

programme.title: Impossible Builds
programme.desc: Bill and Sukie Barber have decided to build a new house in one of their fields using primarily locally sourced materials to fit with their sustainable lifestyle: an ambitious remit in such a secluded corner of the Borders.

programme.title: Impossible Builds
programme.desc: Heather Li and Phil Lines lead jet-setting lives and have dreamt of a wilderness home where they can unwind. Stumbling across their perfect plot on the rugged North West coast of Scotland, they realize conventional house building would be impossible.

programme.title: Impossible Builds
programme.desc: Steve Langstone and his wife Sharon found their perfect plot in some ancient woodland on the side of a lake, where they wanted to construct a home. They found an innovative company that specializes in constructing homes that are technically moveable.

programme.title: Impossible Builds
programme.desc: This episode follows a charismatic petrolhead attempting to build a pioneering, eco-home from a kit of parts to house his ten classic cars.

programme.title: Impossible Builds
programme.desc: Scott, Lisa and their 12-year-old son Archie live in a rented house in the suburbs with no space and individuality that they desire from a home. Instead the family has ordered their new home from a specialist log construction company.

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Architectural designer Dan and interior designer Nina want to build a sleek, unique larch clad family house. But their biggest challenge is to convert a swamp on the plot into a sweeping, biodiverse waterscape with a beautiful natural swimming pond.

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Bereaved Gretta returns from Malaysia to build a single-story, burnt wood and glass pavilion on the land of her sister Mary and brother-in-law Fernando. Also designed by her nephew Carlos, it's a real family affair, a story of recovery and renewal.

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: In South Devon, Joe and Claire's family house will be a giant sculpture inspired by the local landscape, 70 meters long and featuring 34 enormous angled zinc shards jutting out in all directions. But it proves astonishingly complicated to build.

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Young Cumbrian couple Rob and Ruth take on the ruins of a 200-year-old former Blacking Mill, their bold vision to conserve as much of the original structure as possible and within the walls slot in an entirely new sharp edged, modern building.

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Carpenter Olaf creates an oasis for himself and partner Fritha on a small slice of land in West Sussex, squeezed by a mainline railway and a busy road. All they're left with is a very small triangle on which to build their future life together.

programme.title: Million Dollar Dream Home
programme.desc: Eddie and Esperanza are looking for a home that'll cut down their commute. They're visiting some of the most desirable properties in Los Gatos, California. Will they find their dream home?

programme.title: Million Dollar Dream Home
programme.desc: We meet Michael and Kelly who are in search for a luxury home. Viviana is taking them through some the most beautiful mega mansions in Montecito and Santa Barbara, California.

programme.title: Million Dollar Dream Home
programme.sub-title: The Ultimate Home for Art Lovers
programme.desc: George and Bill are looking to cut their commute and move to the city. They're visiting some of the most desirable properties in Palo Alto, California. Will they find their dream home?

programme.title: Million Dollar Dream Home
programme.desc: Today we meet Mish who is on the lookout for her forever home. She is looking for a home perfect for entertaining. But will Viviana be able to find her a place that fits her style?

programme.title: Million Dollar Dream Home
programme.desc: Viviana will help Win find his ultimate dream home. We travel around Santa Barbara in search of a tranquil home in the middle of nature. He needs a peaceful house where he can host his family.

programme.title: Million Dollar Dream Home
programme.desc: We meet John and Laura, a couple who are in search of a family escape luxury home. Viviana takes them through some of the most beautiful mega mansions in Montecito, California.

programme.title: Million Dollar Dream Home
programme.sub-title: Work From Home Meets Garden Goals
programme.desc: Gea and David are looking for more space for indoor offices and outdoor entertaining. They're visiting some of the most desirable properties in Cupertino, California. Will they find their dream home?

programme.title: Million Dollar Dream Home
programme.sub-title: A Villa Escape With Classic Charm
programme.desc: Darren and Meg are relocating from the East Coast. They're looking for better weather year round. They're visiting some of the most desirable properties in Pasadena, California. The want California living with character and a modern twist.

programme.title: Million Dollar Dream Home
programme.sub-title: Trading The East For A West Coast Dream Home
programme.desc: Selena is looking to move closer to Silicon Valley from the East Coast life that she's been living. She's visiting some of the most desirable properties in Saratoga, California. Will she find her dream home?

programme.title: Million Dollar Dream Home
programme.sub-title: A Perfect Home To Start A Family
programme.desc: Kevin and Danielle are looking for a home for their growing family. They're visiting some of the most desirable properties in Pasadena, California. Will they find their dream home?

programme.title: Million Dollar Dream Home
programme.sub-title: Designing A New Life By The Beach
programme.desc: Arica, a designer, and Sean, a contractor, are relocating from San Francisco. They're visiting some of the most desirable properties in Orange County, California. It's important for them to be closer to the beach and have space for their future dogs.

programme.title: Million Dollar Dream Home
programme.desc: We meet Johnny and Jenny who are searching for a luxury home. Viviana is taking them through some of the most beautiful mega mansions in Los Angeles, California. They need a house with the perfect entertaining space.

programme.title: World's Most Secret Homes
programme.desc: A 300-year-old Scottish ruin that's been brought back to life. In the Sierra Nevada mountain, an angular home built from zinc and glass. In Sardinia a stunning waterfront villa. And in Germany, a wartime bunker that lay abandoned for 70 years.

programme.title: World's Most Secret Homes
programme.desc: Head to the Serra Da Mantiqueira Mountains, Brazil, to explore a 2000 square metre, grid shaped family home hidden in nature, then visit a wartime bunker on the Isle of Wight in the UK that's been transformed into an ultra modern home.

programme.title: World's Most Secret Homes
programme.desc: A futuristic villa built on the edge of a lake in Austria. A cabin perched on the edge of a river. A unique beachside house built into the rock, and in rural England a modern mirrored house, designed to blend into and reflect its rural setting.

programme.title: World's Most Secret Homes
programme.desc: A striking modern home has been built out of flint, and then to Switzerland to see a sleek glass residence next to a scenic lake. Deep In the woods in New York State, a far-from-average tree house that serves as a dream getaway for a family of five.

programme.title: World's Most Secret Homes
programme.desc: Venture into the Spanish countryside to explore an enchanting villa. A cliffside retreat that is ultra-modern. In rural Quebec, a cabin that resembles a pair of binoculars perched on a cliff, and finally a woodland retreat in Portugal.

programme.title: World's Most Secret Homes
programme.desc: Explore a palatial cliff-top villa complete with indoor and outdoor pools. A remote cliff-top home deep in West Virginia and then uncover a retreat built into rocks on the rural outskirts of Stockholm.

programme.title: World's Most Secret Homes
programme.desc: A Norwegian mountain cabin that is so remote it is only accessed by helicopter. A family retreat embedded into rocks in the Californian desert. In Brazil, an epic jungle lodge.

programme.title: World's Most Secret Homes
programme.desc: In the Cotswolds, UK, a unique home built into a lake. Lake Ontario, a home that resembles an ocean liner, and then to Portugal, where in land, a ruin has been transformed into a minimalist's dream with a pool concealed in the roof.

programme.title: Alan Cumming's Paradise Homes
programme.desc: Alan escapes to an extraordinary waterside fantasy retreat, a sun-soaked property with endless skylights, and the ultimate floating villa.

programme.title: Alan Cumming's Paradise Homes
programme.sub-title: Good For Nature, Good For Yourself
programme.desc: Alan is struck by the zen and minimalist interior of a mini chateau in the luscious French countryside and experiences an outdoor sauna in the garden of a scenic Swedish getaway.

programme.title: Alan Cumming's Paradise Homes
programme.desc: Alan treks up the scenic hills of Liguria to an elegant subterranean villa and explores a sleek Irish barn house combining modern and traditional styles.

programme.title: Alan Cumming's Paradise Homes
programme.desc: Alan surfs the waters of the Norfolk Broads where a family have created their serene paradise home, and dives into a converted magical playground in the Canadian wilderness.

programme.title: Alan Cumming's Paradise Homes
programme.desc: Alan returns to his rugged roots of the Scottish Highlands to visit an extraordinary hidden getaway and tours an eccentric space-efficient family home in Sicily.

programme.title: Luxury Homes Revealed
programme.desc: Our first home is located in Palm Springs, then we get an exclusive peek inside an incredible private estate in the Hollywood Hills. Next we travel to Hampton, Australia and then we take a tour of a resort-like home in Park City, Utah.

programme.title: Luxury Homes Revealed
programme.desc: Our first home is in Brentwood, California, a bright and airy design. Then we tour a masterpiece in sunny California. We travel to Australia for a look at a renovated modern farmhouse. Lastly, we tour a six-bedroom masterpiece in Park City, Utah.

programme.title: Luxury Homes Revealed
programme.desc: Our first home is located in Hancock Park, a recently renovated 1928 estate. Then we tour an English-inspired manor in Bel Air. Afterwards we travel to Australia, and then lastly we take a tour of a massive home in the Hollywood Hills.

programme.title: Luxury Homes Revealed
programme.desc: Our first home is located in Hawaii, a beachfront property with views of coastal waters. Then we check out an oceanfront home in Honolulu. We travel to Australia to see a family home. Then we tour a $30 million compound that sits right on the beach.

programme.title: World's Most Secret Homes

programme.desc: A 300-year-old Scottish ruin that's been brought back to life. In the Sierra Nevada mountain, an angular home built from zinc and glass. In Sardinia a stunning waterfront villa. And in Germany, a wartime bunker that lay abandoned for 70 years.

programme.title: Backyard Builds
programme.desc: Couple Farzana & Reyan have maxed out all living space in their city-sized bungalow, leaving no room for Reyan's at-home stained glass business or Farzana's writing. Brian and Sarah build Reyan a spacious studio and construct an inspiring deck area.

programme.title: Backyard Builds
programme.sub-title: A Space For Dancing and Hanging
programme.desc: Susan's energetic son, Harrison, has no space to dance at home without getting under his mom's feet. Brian and Sarah build a hang out / dance space for Harrison and create a California-inspired pool and lounge area for Susan.

programme.title: Backyard Builds
programme.desc: Breanna & Nick's life revolves around socializing and physical activity. Brian and Sarah convert an old, underused shed into a beautiful, zen'd out yoga studio and create backyard partying, lounging and dining areas.

programme.title: Backyard Builds
programme.desc: Brian and Sarah create a backyard for Pam & Kevin that matches their lush forest surrounding complete with a double wrap around deck and a hot tub. The team also build a workshop for Kevin to perfect his craft of leather tooling.

programme.title: Backyard Builds
programme.desc: Love is large but space is tight for Lara & Mike and their 5 children. Brian and Sarah free up space by building an activity-packed tree house and recreation court for the children, with grilling, dining and lounging areas for the whole family.

programme.title: Backyard Builds
programme.desc: Janet's love for her nine grandkids is endless. But so is the chaos they bring inside her home when they come visit. To take that energy outside, Brian and Sarah build a fun kid-zone in the backyard, but also a sophisticated adult area for Janet.

programme.title: Backyard Builds
programme.desc: Medical doctors and busy parents, Cynthia and Boon miss their camping adventures. Brian and Sarah perform some backyard surgery, and build them a cozy campground retreat so they can go straight from the ER to getting some R&R right in their own yard.

programme.title: Backyard Builds
programme.desc: Ex-city slickers, Lauren and Mark, miss their friends and family from the city. Brian and Sarah create a country retreat with an outdoor pad, indoor dining lounge, and a chicken coop. When Lauren and Mark's friends visit, they'll never want to leave.

programme.title: Backyard Builds

programme.title: Backyard Builds

programme.title: Backyard Builds
programme.desc: Janet's love for her nine grandkids is endless. But so is the chaos they bring inside her home when they come visit. To take that energy outside, Brian and Sarah build a fun kid-zone in the backyard, but also a sophisticated adult area for Janet.

programme.title: From Scratch
programme.desc: In the middle of the woods next to a shiny lake sits a house made of glass that is home to one of the most unique restaurants. David has a week to create a healthy meal all gathered within ten miles of the woods and cook it in an outdoor kitchen.

programme.title: From Scratch

programme.title: From Scratch
programme.desc: Mile High City isn't the steak and potatoes town it was and much of it has to do with James Beard award winning chef Jennifer Jasinski and her restaurant Rioja. David learns fly fishing, harvests a lamb, mills wheat and farms within the city limits.

programme.title: From Scratch
programme.desc: David road trips around Kansas and Missouri on a mission from James Beard-nominated Chefs Johnny Leach and Helen Jo Leach at The Town Company, located in the Hotel Kansas City. Local farmers, ancient grist millers and artisanal butchers guide David.

programme.title: Extraordinary Places to Eat

programme.title: Extraordinary Places to Eat

programme.title: Extraordinary Places to Eat
programme.desc: Chris Bavin takes Fred to the New Forest, where fresh local produce is king. Featuring wood-fired cooking, pork pies and a country pub serving the perfect Ploughman's.

programme.title: Extraordinary Places to Eat
programme.desc: Fred Sirieix is heading to Bristol for a winter treat in the company of Michel Roux Junior. Michel can't wait to show Fred his favourites, from the perfect French bistro to a festive countryside escape and Jamaican jerk turkey.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver Cooks Italy
programme.desc: Jamie tracks down some fast, fresh and easy recipes, featuring tasty minty pea tortiglioni and gnocchi with roasted tomato sauce and rocket pesto.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver Cooks Italy
programme.desc: Jamie visits the pizza capital of Italy to learn recipes from the locals and tries acquapazza, fish poached in crazy water.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver Cooks Italy
programme.desc: Jamie heads for one of the hottest food destinations in Italy, making hand-crafted pasta, a special seafood stew, and the region's answer to paella.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver Cooks Italy
programme.desc: Jamie visits Basilicata in search of some old school Italian recipes and meets a family of shepherds and an 89-year-old macaroni master.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver Cooks Italy
programme.desc: Jamie gets a masterclass in Sicilian sweets and cooking up an epic tuna, prawn and pistachio spaghetti. There's also some chocolaty cannoli.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver Cooks Italy
programme.desc: Jamie is high up in the Italian Alps cooking some great comfort food, including dumplings, mind-blowing macaroni cheese, and a classic red wine and sausage risotto.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver Cooks Italy
programme.sub-title: Best of Salina's Land and Sea
programme.desc: Jamie returns to cooking the food he loves the most, getting right to heart of the Italian kitchen.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver Cooks Italy
programme.desc: Jamie is taking spaghetti bolognese to the next level and re-creating a mind-blowing Jewish-Italian recipe. There's also beef stew with pappardelle.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver Cooks the Mediterranean
programme.desc: Jamie heads to Marseille in the south of France. Inspired by their stunning seafood, the traditional flavors of Provence, and the multicultural dishes being served up there, he cooks up a steak with a twist and a sublime courgette and olive tart.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver Cooks the Mediterranean
programme.desc: Jamie's soaking up inspiration in Spain with tapas, flavor-charged rice dishes, and spectacular seafood. He cooks a stunning pork chop with peppers and alioli and a surf and turf skewer of prawns, mushrooms, and local ham.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver Cooks the Mediterranean
programme.desc: Jamie heads to Greece, to the island of Skopelos, starting on the mainland in Thessaloniki. Inspired by meze plates and traditional island cuisine he cooks up a smoky aubergine flatbread and a lemony chargrilled chicken with tzatziki and rice.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver Cooks the Mediterranean
programme.desc: Jamie explores Tunisia to meet traditional couscous makers, eat delicious street food, and get invited to cook with local chefs on a beach. Inspired by everything he sees, Jamie cooks crispy prawn parcels and a chicken and merguez sausage stew.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Street Food Asia
programme.desc: Luke arrives in Saigon for a breakfast of iced coffee with condensed milk and a vermicelli noodle dish. Later, he visits his favourite Pho restaurant, then cooks up some spicy Chilli Crab Claws.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Street Food Asia
programme.desc: Luke continues his street food journey in Bangkok where he searches for the best Tom Yum soup and entertains the locals with a rare Thai delicacy.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Street Food Asia
programme.sub-title: Malaysian Flying Noodles and Pork Bone Soup

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Street Food Asia

programme.desc: Frankie Celenza takes a LOT of bites out of the big apple as he samples warm pillowy bagels & schmear, grabs pierogies at an old school Ukrainian spot, gets the scoop on innovative ice cream flavors, and slurps up some real ramen.

programme.title: Worth the Hype
programme.sub-title: Quail, Alligator Sausage, & Gumbo in New Orleans
programme.desc: Frankie stops by New Orleans to chow down on lacquered quail, a beast of a Po Boy, and Creole Beef Daube.

programme.title: Worth the Hype
programme.desc: Frankie explores Hawaii's O'ahu island to get nourished from fresh tuna poke, heartfelt Pho, and hand-pounded poi.

programme.title: Worth the Hype
programme.sub-title: Smash Burgers & A Slice in Los Angeles
programme.desc: Frankie Celenza heads to all the buzzworthy spots in LA - cooking up calamari steak at a classic Mexican joint, tasting taro root donuts with fun flavors, grabbing a guanciale slice, and hopping on the line to make cheesy scrumptious smash burgers.

programme.title: Worth the Hype
programme.sub-title: Chiles, Burgers, & Tenderloins in Santa Fe
programme.desc: Frankie heads to Santa Fe investigating New Mexican cuisines eating green chili burgers, poblano chile stews, and sweet blue-corn pancakes.

programme.title: Worth the Hype
programme.sub-title: Salmon Tacos, Meatballs, & Perogies in Las Vegas
programme.desc: Frankie dazzles in Las Vegas where he enjoys crunchy salmon skin tacos, an homage to afterschool snacks, and the dish that put Martha Stewart on the map.

programme.title: Worth the Hype
programme.desc: Frankie visits Nashville for smoky BBQ Pork, hand-pulled noodles, a luxe burger, and hand-pulled noodles.

programme.title: Worth the Hype

programme.sub-title: Secret Family Recipies in Ho Chi Minh
